name: Stir Fried Rice Noodles with Tofu & Egg (Pad Thai)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 pound Thai flat rice noodles (bahn pho)
1/2 pound firm tofu, cut into small cubes
1/2 cup very thinly sliced chicken breast meat
1/4 pound shrimp, peeled and cut in half the long way
3 tablespoons dry-roasted salted peanuts, chopped fine
2 teaspoons red chili paste with garlic
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the tamarind sauce (recipe follows) and set it aside to cool.
2. Soak the noodles in warm water until pliable, about 15 minutes, then drain and set aside. Place the noodles in boiling water and cook just until the water returns to a boil. Drain again.
3. Heat oil for deep-frying to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C) and deep-fry tofu. Pat tofu dry on paper towels first to prevent spattering. Drain and set aside.
4. Heat peanut oil in a large wok, add garlic, and stir-fry for a few minutes. Add chicken, shrimp, drained noodles, beaten eggs, and deep-fried tofu. Toss well and stir-fry over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes until noodles are hot and tender.
5. Add remaining ingredients except garnishes and stir-fry for a few more minutes.
6. Serve with garnishes on a platter. Serves 4-6.
Notes: To make shrimp powder, grind dried shrimp in a food processor or blender. Peanut oil can be reduced by half.
